dict.string: YT Grab — download a video or audio track from a shared or pasted link through a yt-dlp API. - Uses the Share Sheet link if provided, otherwise the Clipboard when it looks like a URL, otherwise asks you to paste one. - Lets you choose Video (with a quality) or Solo audio. - Posts the request; on error it shows an alert, otherwise it saves the file (video to Photos, audio to Files).

Shortcuts generated by Shortcuts Playground. May contain mistakes. Always check the shortcut's actions first.
This shortcut was created via the following user prompt:
> Build a shortcut named "YT Grab" that accepts Share Sheet input (URLs / Text / Safari web pages); if Shortcut Input has a value use it as the link, otherwise use the clipboard when it matches a URL pattern, otherwise ask for a link. Choose from a menu Video (with a quality sub-menu) or Solo audio, POST url/mode/height as JSON to a yt-dlp API, and either show the returned error or save the file (video to Photos, audio to Files).
Decide which link to use: - If the Share Sheet passed input, use it as the link (URLToUse). - Otherwise read the Clipboard and test it against a URL pattern.
- Count is the number of URL matches found in the clipboard text. - If Count is greater than 0, use the Clipboard as the link. - Otherwise ask the user to paste a link.

Choose what to download: - Video sets Mode to video, then asks for a quality and builds the request body (BodyJSON). - Solo audio sets Mode to audio, then asks for a bitrate and builds the request body with audioQuality.

Pick a video quality. Each option builds the JSON body with the link and the chosen height (2160/1440/1080/720/480/360/240/144). yt-dlp requests the best stream at or below that height, so 2160p doubles as "best available".

Pick an audio bitrate. Each option builds the JSON body with the link and the chosen audioQuality: - Migliore disponibile sends "0" (best available VBR). - 320/256/192/128/96 kbps send the matching "NNNK" value (server whitelist; anything else falls back to best).
Send the request: - Posts the built JSON body to the yt-dlp API. - Detects the response as a dictionary and reads any error value.
- If the response has an error value, show it in an alert. - Otherwise the response is the downloaded file, ready to save.
- If Mode is video, save the file to Photos. - Otherwise save the audio file to Files.
